title: atriplex patula var. subspicata
intro: Atriplex patula var. subspicata, also known as Spreading Orach, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States, characterized by its reddish-green leaves and stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, wounds, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves and stems of the Spreading Orach have been used to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from fever and skin irritations, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Atriplex patula var. subspicata include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to harness its medicinal benefits.
